meta_title: For Founders: SaaS pricing to Solve tutorial hell (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome tutorial hell. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: SaaS pricing to Solve tutorial hell (Checklist)
content.seo_description: Stop building for free. Master SaaS pricing strategies and escape tutorial hell with this intermediate guide using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Escape tutorial hell by shifting from code-first to value-first. Implement a tiered SaaS pricing model that forces you to define clear user personas. By automating your billing infrastructure and deployment with Claude Code, you transform abstract tutorials into a production-ready engine that captures revenue from day one.
content.intro:
You are drowning in a sea of 'To-Do list' apps and generic clones. You know the syntax, but you're trapped in the infinite loop of tutorial hell, building features nobody pays for while your competitors capture the market. The difference between an amateur developer and a SaaS founder isn't the stack—it's the relentless focus on the value exchange.

It is time to kill the hobbyist mindset. We are going to treat your code as a commercial asset. By integrating strategic pricing early, you force yourself to build for a specific customer profile, effectively ending the cycle of aimless development and launching you into the realm of real-world profitability.

steps.title: Define Your Value Metric
steps.description: Identify the unit of value your customers pay for. If you don't know what you're charging for, you haven't built a SaaS; you've built a utility.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my project idea and suggest three distinct pricing tiers based on usage-based, feature-gated, and per-seat models, focusing on maximizing MRR for early-stage founders.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create a pricing.md file outlining the value metrics and tier structure based on the current codebase scope.'

steps.title: Architecting the Stripe Integration
steps.description: Don't write billing logic from scratch. Use established patterns to handle subscriptions, webhooks, and customer sessions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a secure Stripe integration flow for a Next.js application, including webhook handlers for subscription events and automatic user provisioning.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add stripe-sdk --task 'Implement the boilerplate for Stripe session creation in the API routes.'

steps.title: Implementing Feature Gating
steps.description: Ensure that your paid tiers actually restrict access. This is where your pricing becomes enforceable code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a middleware structure that checks the user's subscription status against their database record before allowing access to premium API endpoints.
steps.claude_code_command: claude refactor middleware.ts --task 'Add a subscription check to the /api/premium route group.'

steps.title: Automating Revenue Analytics
steps.description: If you cannot measure it, you cannot scale it. Embed tracking early to monitor conversion rates from free to paid.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a schema for tracking user billing events and conversion funnels within our PostgreSQL database.
steps.claude_code_command: claude generate migration --task 'Add subscription_status and plan_id columns to the users table.'

steps.title: Deploying the Monetization Engine
steps.description: Prepare your infrastructure to handle real traffic and payments securely.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review my current deployment configuration and suggest hardening measures for handling sensitive billing data and environment variables.
steps.claude_code_command: claude deploy --check-env --task 'Verify that all Stripe API keys and webhooks are correctly configured in production secrets.'

pro_tips: Never offer a 'Free Forever' plan; offer a time-bound trial to force value discovery.
Charge by the 'outcome' your user achieves, not by the storage space or API calls you consume.
Use Stripe's Customer Portal to reduce churn; don't build your own billing management UI.
If you're not getting rejected by 20% of your leads, your pricing is too low.
Automate your dunning emails immediately; failed credit card payments are the silent killer of early-stage SaaS.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing is live, shift your focus entirely to distribution. Build a landing page, capture emails, and get your first 10 paying customers before adding a single new feature.
content.conversion_block: You have the roadmap, the tools, and the strategy to stop being a student and start being a founder. Stop writing code for the sake of code and start building a business that scales. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
